negative_cases.why_wrong: Logic Vector Reduction Flag: forces the main output to zero, so coarse compilation passes but behavioral stimulus points fail.
Logic Vector Reduction Flag: uses a shifted or inverted decision condition that can pass one mode but fails boundary cases.
Logic Vector Reduction Flag: mishandles reset or initialization, passing steady-state samples but failing startup or recovery checks.
Logic Vector Reduction Flag: keeps the main datapath plausible while reporting the wrong metric value.
Logic Vector Reduction Flag: uses a wrong gain or scale factor, leaving shape plausible while calibrated checks fail.
